_meta.description: Golden callee (outbound) dataset for direct-callee recall benchmarking
_meta.usage: Run scripts/benchmark/run_caller_recall.sh run --project-path ... --direction callees to evaluate
_meta.normalization: Chunk IDs normalized (line ranges stripped via evaluation/metrics.py:normalize_chunk_id)
_meta.format: target_chunk_id → list of expected direct-callee chunk IDs
_meta.relevance_scale: All entries in expected_callees are grade=2 (direct callee confirmed by code reading)

queries.description: run_resolvers calls sorted(), resolver.available(), resolver.resolve(), dict.get()

PyanResolver.resolve calls prepare_scoped_files, the shared gather/scope/validate preamble extracted for PyanResolver, LibCSTResolver, and LSPResolver. gather_py_files, scope_to_indexed_files, and validate_py_files are now called from inside prepare_scoped_files (2-hop), not directly from resolve

upgrade_call_edge calls graph.edges[...].update; caller_id and callee_id are each normalized via normalize_path first (#47, added after this golden's original authoring) so Windows backslash ids don't cause a KeyError against forward-slash graph ids
